current: Let kappa=2(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c satisfying r_mu,n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu,n r_e,n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, define zeta(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{1/2-lambda/2,c-lambda,a+lambda/(2gamma)} with the closed-form optimizer in lem:clip-balance-exponent. Define r_plug=kappa zeta(a,c,gamma), q_n=n^{-lambda_*} capped at 1/4, and r_feas=min{r_star(alpha,gamma),r_plug}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, set q_n to a fixed value below the floor and r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
proposed: Let r0=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha). For gamma>0 and certified polynomial nuisance exponents a,c satisfying r_mu,n=O(n^{-a}) and r_mu,n r_e,n=O(n^{-c}), c>=1/2, define the additive feasible exponent r_tilde_feas(a,c,gamma)=sup_{lambda>=0} min{r0(1-lambda), c-lambda, a+lambda/(2gamma)}. Let lambda_* be the closed-form optimizer in lem:additive-clip-balance-exponent and set q_n=min{1/4,n^{-lambda_*}}. Define r_feas(alpha,gamma,a,c)=min{r_star(alpha,gamma), r_tilde_feas(a,c,gamma)}. For gamma=0 under a strict-overlap floor, set q_n to a fixed value below the floor and r_feas=r_star=(1+alpha)/(2+alpha).
reason: The exact drift identity in lem:clip-bias gives a global second-order feasible drift term. Under the frozen L2 nuisance assumptions this drift enters the basic regret inequality additively, while only the empirical-process term is exponentiated by the margin self-bound. Therefore the old formula kappa*min{1/2-lambda/2,c-lambda,a+lambda/(2gamma)} overstates the nuisance-bias part of the feasible rate. The corrected definition uses the actual exponents of the additive master bound: the empirical exponent is r0(1-lambda), while the two drift exponents remain c-lambda and a+lambda/(2gamma).
